summary: Support firewatch skill reads a support thread and SLA policy, detects sentiment, SLA breach, and churn risk signals, and emits an escalation packet only when warranted. Routes to human approval inbox without paging, reassigning, or notifying customers.
observations: Skill reads thread (array of customer/agent turns) and sla_policy (response_time_minutes, resolution_hours) as typed inputs
Sentiment detection uses keyword analysis on customer turns: positive, neutral, negative, very_negative
SLA breach detection compares first-response gap to sla_policy.response_time_minutes policy clock
Churn risk detection identifies cancellation/competitor language: none, low, medium, high
Escalation packet (needed, priority, context) emitted only when warranted — routes to human approval inbox, pages nobody, reassigns nothing, notifies no customer
Each signal carries _evidence grounding it to a specific thread turn or policy clock

Harness passes 3/3: sealed_escalation_flagged (very_negative, sla_breach=true, churn_risk=medium → escalation needed, high priority), refused_no_escalation_healthy_thread (positive, no breach, no churn → no escalation), stop_missing_required_inputs (empty turns → failure)

dogfood.input: Support thread with frustrated customer (3 turns, negative sentiment), SLA policy with 60min response time, agent responded after 145 minutes
dogfood.output: signals.sentiment=very_negative, signals.sla_breach=true, signals.churn_risk=medium, escalation.needed=true, escalation.priority=high, escalation.context includes SLA breach and sentiment evidence
